title: Aureline native file-affordance contract cases
description: Boundary schema for worked native-file-affordance cases frozen in docs/ux/native_file_affordance_contract.md. These case records exist to keep native open/save dialogs, reveal-in-system-shell, open-from-terminal, file association entry, drag/drop, clipboard, and browser-originated open flows from becoming per-platform side effects. Each case must make target identity, trust scope, write posture, and safe-preview status inspectable and traceable to canonical object identity and command/intent refs. Raw file bodies, secrets, credentials, raw provider payloads, raw deep-link bodies, and raw browser URLs never cross this boundary; cases carry typed vocabulary and opaque refs only. Literal OS targets may be included as input strings solely to exercise identity binding and wrong-target prevention behavior.

native_file_affordance_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this native-file-affordance case export. Bumped only on breaking payload changes.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for this contract's fixture record.

affordance_class.description: Primary native affordance being exercised by the case.

target_kind.description: High-level target kind for case grouping. Detailed path classes live in external_path_classes.

save_token_posture.description: Whether a save-target token is minted now, deferred until first write, or unavailable (which blocks writes).

safe_preview_trust_class.description: Optional safe-preview trust class when the case involves preview/copy/export semantics.

command_id_ref.description: Stable command id ref backing the affordance path (e.g. cmd:workspace.open_file).

object_identity_ref.description: Opaque canonical object identity ref the product binds to after resolution.

system_open_target_packet_ref.description: Optional ref to a system_open_target_packet record (by packet id) that captures literal vs canonical binding.

deep_link_intent_ref.description: Optional ref to a deep_link_intent_record when the affordance originates from a link/URI entry path.

browser_handoff_packet_ref.description: Optional ref to a browser_handoff_packet_record when the affordance leaves/returns from a system browser.

save_target_token_ref.description: Optional ref to a save_target_token_packet (by packet id or token id) backing write posture disclosure.

write_review_sheet_ref.description: Optional ref to a write_review_sheet_record when a write-like action requires review/retargeting.

suspicious_content_ref.description: Optional ref to a suspicious-content case/packet that drives safe-preview and copy/export labeling.

decode_recovery_review_ref.description: Optional ref to a decode-recovery review record when text fidelity affects preview/copy/save semantics.

outside_expected_scope.description: True when the literal target resolves outside the expected workspace/profile/trust scope and requires review before admission.

alias_disclosure_required.description: True when aliases/collisions must be disclosed (UNC vs mapped drive, case variants, normalization variants, symlink/junction chains) before a write-like action.

must_preserve_identity.description: All cases are about identity preservation; this is pinned true as an invariant.

must_preserve_safe_preview_status.description: True when the affordance must keep safe-preview/suspicious-content posture inspectable across handoff.

must_not_write_without_token.description: When true, the case asserts a save-target token (or an explicit token-unavailable posture) exists before any durable write.

__fixture__.description: Optional metadata for human review tooling; not used by runtime consumers.

audit_event_id.description: Reuse of the platform audit-event vocabulary for affordance-driven case evidence.
